Title: Fine-Tuning the Function of Farnesene Synthases for Selective Synthesis of Farnesene Stereoisomers
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type I terpene synthase; sesquiterpene synthase; DDxxD and DSE metal-binding motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous expression in engineered yeast strain Sc027
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ionization of farnesyl pyrophosphate to a farnesyl cation and pyrophosphate, followed by deprotonation at C13 to form β-farnesene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key building block for biosynthesis of vitamin E
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for β-farnesene formation; engineering target for yield improvement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-farnesene accumulation; engineered variants markedly increased β-farnesene yield in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: galactose induction during shake-flask cultivation in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae Sc027; site-directed mutagenesis; GC-MS metabolite profiling; purified enzyme kinetic assay; molecular dynamics simulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wild-type AaFS converts FPP to β-farnesene as the main product. 2. Structure-guided engineering identified L326I and M433I as high-activity variants, with L326I increasing β-farnesene yield from 450.65 to 3877.42 mg/L and showing 3.3-fold higher catalytic efficiency than WT. 3. W299 is critical for product stereoselectivity, and Y402 controls diversion toward monocyclic products including α-bisabolol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent metabolic-engineering target for microbial production of β-farnesene as a vitamin E precursor; beneficial mutations such as L326I, M433I, and Q142H can be used to boost yield, while W299 and Y402 can be edited to alter product selectivity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Certain mutations reduce catalytic efficiency or divert flux to non-target sesquiterpenes such as α-farnesene, α-bisabolol, zingiberenol, β-bisabolene, and isoitalicene
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic site contains three Mg2+ ions coordinated by DDxxD and DSE motifs; D471N reduced activity whereas S475T retained activity, suggesting preference for DSE/DTE-like coordination in AaFS

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous expression in engineered yeast strain Sc027
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered AaFS variant converting FPP to β-farnesene with improved catalytic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key building block for biosynthesis of vitamin E
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: improved terminal-step catalyst for β-farnesene production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases β-farnesene yield to 3877.42 mg/L, 8.6-fold above wild type
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: galactose induction during shake-flask cultivation in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous yeast production assay; GC-MS quantification; steady-state kinetics; molecular dynamics simulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. L326I is the best-performing AaFS variant for β-farnesene production. 2. It decreased Km and increased kcat/Km to 40.4 × 10^-3 μM^-1·s^-1, 3.3-fold above WT. 3. MD simulation suggests pocket reshaping via L323 side-chain flip enhances hydrophobic interaction with the substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme-engineering mutation for industrial microbial production of β-farnesene/vitamin E precursor
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation reshapes hydrophobic pocket and stabilizes productive substrate conformation

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type I terpene synthase; sesquiterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous expression in engineered yeast strain Sc027
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ionization of FPP to a farnesyl cation followed by deprotonation at C4 to form α-farnesene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for α-farnesene stereoisomer formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces α-farnesene as the sole product in heterologous yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: galactose induction during shake-flask cultivation in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae Sc027; GC-MS metabolite profiling; comparative molecular dynamics simulation; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fsso is an α-farnesene synthase that converts FPP to α-farnesene as sole product. 2. Comparative analysis with AaFS identified the residue corresponding to AaFS W299 as a determinant of stereoisomer selectivity. 3. Mutation of Fsso C293 to tryptophan switched product outcome toward non-native β-farnesene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful complementary synthase scaffold for engineering selective α-farnesene or β-farnesene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation can alter stereoselectivity and produce non-native products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper does not clearly state the source organism name in the provided text; retained as NA to avoid unsupported inference
